skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: A study was done on the weights of various species of birds at a sanctuary. A random sample of birds was captured and weighed, ensuring each bird was weighed only once. The sample contained 240 sparrows, of which 45% weighed more than 30 grams. Which of the following conclusions is best supported by the sample data?

answerOptions.content: Approximately 45% of all sparrows in the sanctuary weigh more than 30 grams.
Approximately 45% of all birds in the sanctuary weigh more than 30 grams.
The average weight of all birds in the sanctuary is approximately 30 grams.
The majority of all birds in the sanctuary weigh less than 30 grams.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The sample statistic supports an inference about the population from which the sample was drawn — in this case, sparrows in the sanctuary.

Choices B, C, and D generalize beyond sparrows to all birds, which is unsupported by a sparrow-only sample.
